rationale: The only evidence is a single DOI-level bibliography match (reference index 42) with no in-text citation context or title string, so the citation’s function in the citing paper is unclear. The two papers do share eye-tracking/dynamic behavioral modeling elements, but they are in different substantive domains (conjoint search-and-choice vs. video-ad emotion/zapping), which does not by itself show clear substantive use. Given this sparse and mixed evidence, it is not safe to confidently drop or keep.
